macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates, Driving Oil Prices to 1-Month High and Raising Fed Rate-Hike Odds
items.summary: Renewed military hostilities between the US and Iran, including strikes on Iranian infrastructure and disruptions to Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic, pushed WTI crude up 2.74% and gasoline prices to a 7-week high, fueling inflation fears and increasing the probability of a July Fed rate hike despite June inflation cooling to 3.5%.

items.title: Major US Indices Slide as Chip Stocks Selloff Deepens and AI Rally Faces Sustainability Questions
items.summary: US stock index futures slid Friday as chip stocks faced their steepest weekly decline in over a year, with the S&P 500 down 1.01% and the Nikkei 225 plunging 4.34%, while investors reassess the AI-driven surge amid weak Netflix earnings and rotation out of recent rally winners.

items.title: Fed Policymakers Swell for Rate Hike as Cleveland Fed President Hammack Joins Inflation Debate
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack added to a growing chorus of policymakers arguing rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at Chairman Kevin Warsh's upcoming July meeting, though rates are still widely seen on hold initially.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Stock Volatility
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot as the world's most valuable company with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia dropped 3.5%, while Alphabet fell 2.17% ahead of its earnings report expected to sway the market-leading AI trade.

items.title: Wall Street Banks Post Record Earnings as AI Boom Drives Trading Revenue, While Regional Banks Deliver Q2 Gains
items.summary: Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley reported record quarterly revenue and profit driven by surging equities trading (up 69% for Morgan Stanley), while regional banks like U.S. Bancorp and PNC Financial delivered broad Q2 gains despite Middle East war concerns.

items.title: US Import Prices Unexpectedly Rise in June; Consumer Sentiment Hits Five-Month High Before Gasoline Spike
items.summary: US import prices unexpectedly rose in June, and consumer sentiment increased to a five-month high in July, though the improvement is likely temporary as renewed US-Iran conflict raises gasoline prices and threatens to reverse June's inflation cooling to 3.5%.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $63K Amid Middle East Tensions and Risk-Off Selling
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 1.4% to trade just under $63,600 on July 17, continuing a second straight day of losses as Middle East tensions triggered fresh risk-off selling, with support now focused at $60,000.

items.title: Ethereum Slips 3.9% to $1,846 as CLARITY Act Doubts Weigh on Crypto Market
items.summary: Ethereum fell 3.90% over the past 24 hours to trade around $1,846, while Polymarket traders cut odds of the Clarity Act's passage to record lows as Senate delays continue.

items.title: Nigeria Establishes Virtual Asset Commission with New Executive Order
items.summary: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu signed an executive order creating a regulatory framework for virtual assets and establishing a Virtual Asset Committee chaired by the Central Bank of Nigeria.

items.title: FTX to Distribute $900 Million in Fifth Creditor Payout on July 31
items.summary: FTX will launch its fifth round of creditor distributions on July 31, 2026, with approximately $900 million going to eligible creditors, marking a major step in its bankruptcy repayment process.

items.title: Dutch Crypto Exchange Knaken Collapses After MiCA Rules Trigger Fund Losses
items.summary: The collapse of Dutch cryptocurrency platform Knaken has raised fresh concerns about custodial crypto safety after millions in customer funds went missing following MiCA regulatory triggers.

items.title: Ostium Loses $24 Million in Oracle Exploit Attack
items.summary: DeFi protocol Ostium suffered at least $24 million in losses due to an oracle exploit, highlighting ongoing security vulnerabilities in decentralized finance infrastructure.

items.title: BONK Governance Attackers Move 1.626 Trillion Tokens to Centralized Exchanges
items.summary: Wallets tied to the BONK governance incident have transferred a cumulative 1.626 trillion BONK tokens (worth ~$5.58 million) to centralized exchanges, with another 400 billion moved to Coinbase recently.

items.title: Fidelity Launches Stablecoin FIDD on Platform and Crypto Exchanges
items.summary: Fidelity is launching its own stablecoin, FIDD, which will be available on Fidelity's platform and major crypto exchanges, expanding the asset manager's digital currency offerings.

items.title: Uniswap Proposes Burning All New Protocol Fees via UNI Token
items.summary: Uniswap submitted two governance proposals stating that all new protocol fees will be directed into the UNI burn mechanism, potentially increasing token scarcity.

items.title: Grayscale Updates Solana Staking ETF to Pay Quarterly Cash Rewards
items.summary: Grayscale filed a prospectus supplement on July 17 to introduce mandatory quarterly cash distributions for its Grayscale Solana Staking ETF (GSOL), making it the first staking ETF to pay cash rewards.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases ChatGPT-5.6 Family (Sol, Terra, Luna) with Enhanced Reasoning
items.summary: OpenAI launched the ChatGPT-5.6 model family, including Sol, Terra, and Luna variants, featuring stronger multi-step reasoning and improved template adherence, rolling out globally within 24 hours.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion-parameter open-weight model claiming performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and rivaling top U.S. systems.

items.title: Anthropic Deploys Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Fable and Mythos Models
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its Fable and Mythos frontier models following a federal export control review.

items.title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemini 2.5 with 10M Token Context Window
items.summary: Google DeepMind launched Gemini 2.5 on July 15, featuring a 10 million token context window, 92% MMLU score, and native video/code reasoning for long-context enterprise applications.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets in Hardware Development
items.summary: Apple filed a federal lawsuit against OpenAI and two executives alleging theft of confidential information to develop competing devices, accusing OpenAI's chief hardware officer Tang Tan and technical staff member Chang Liu.

items.title: Mistral AI Releases Mistral Large 3 with 2M Token Context Window
items.summary: Mistral AI launched Mistral Large 3 on July 15, featuring a 2 million token context window and a 92% MMLU score.

items.title: Google Makes Veo 3 AI Video Creation Tool Widely Available
items.summary: Google expanded access to its most powerful AI video creator, Veo 3, making it available for all users on its Vertex AI platform.

items.title: IBM Research Unveils Groundbreaking Analog AI Chip for Deep Neural Networks
items.summary: IBM Research announced a new analog AI chip demonstrating remarkable efficiency and accuracy in performing complex computations for deep neural networks.

items.title: Nvidia Announces Cosmos 3 Edge Model and Expands Physical AI Ecosystem in Japan
items.summary: Nvidia unveiled Cosmos 3 Edge and expanded its physical AI ecosystems in Japan, partnering with Fujitsu to develop physical AI using Nvidia technology.

items.title: US Senate Passes AI Export Control Act Restricting Advanced Chip Sales
items.summary: The US Senate passed the AI Export Control Act on July 14, restricting sales of advanced AI chips and model weights above 100 billion parameters to certain countries, with enforcement starting September 1, 2026.

items.title: MIT Researchers Achieve 96% on MATH Benchmark with New Reasoning Model
items.summary: MIT researchers published a new reasoning architecture achieving 96% on the MATH benchmark, surpassing previous state-of-the-art by 11 percentage points using symbolic verification layers.

items.title: Hugging Face Releases Transformers 5.0 with Native Multimodal Support
items.summary: Hugging Face launched Transformers 5.0 on July 16, adding native multimodal model support and unified APIs for vision-language and audio-text models compatible with over 120,000 community models.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense with Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity for AI
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with data center capacity worth billions to run AI models, according to Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: TSMC Plans Additional $100 Billion U.S. Investment Amid AI Infrastructure Surge
items.summary: Taiwan's TSMC announced plans to invest another $100 billion expanding U.S. manufacturing capacity, driven by record earnings from global AI infrastructure demand.

items.title: New York Bans New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers fueling artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Confirms Obsidian Returning to Fallout Universe for New Game
items.summary: Bethesda officially confirmed that Obsidian Entertainment, the studio behind Fallout: New Vegas, is returning to the post-apocalyptic universe to develop a new game, fulfilling fan requests dating back to 2010.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Shadows Cracked: Denuvo Protection Removed
items.summary: The game Assassin's Creed Shadows has been cracked by voices38, allowing it to launch without Denuvo anti-tamper workarounds, marking the first time this specific protection has been broken for the title released in March 2025.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 and Title Update 1.4.1.0 Announced for July 21
items.summary: EA and Battlefield Studios confirmed that Battlefield 6 Season 4 will arrive on July 21 across PC, PS5, and Xbox, coinciding with the release of Title Update 1.4.1.0.

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Mobile Global Release Canceled, China Service Ends September 2026
items.summary: LightSpeed Studios and Square Enix confirmed the global release of Final Fantasy XIV Mobile has been canceled, with the Chinese version set to remain online only until September 30, 2026.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Tops 3 Million Copies Sold
items.summary: Ubisoft announced that the remake Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has surpassed 3 million copies sold just one week after launch, with New Game Plus confirmed to be in development.

items.title: Gallipoli Tactical WWI Shooter Rescheduled for August 20 Release
items.summary: Blackmill Games announced a new release date for its tactical World War I multiplayer shooter Gallipoli, now locked for August 20 on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero Version 3.1 Update Set for July 29 to Mark Second Anniversary
items.summary: MiHoYo revealed that Zenless Zone Zero will receive its Version 3.1 update on July 29, 2026, featuring new agents, a permanent mode, and anniversary rewards across PC, PS5, mobile, and Xbox.

items.title: T1 Dominates HLE 2-0 in MSI Championship Match
items.summary: In major esports news, T1 swept HLE with a 2-0 victory in the MSI championship, securing a decisive win over their opponents.

items.title: Skyrim Reaches 65 Million Copies Sold, Tying The Witcher 3
items.summary: Bethesda Game Studios reported that The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim has sold 65 million copies 15 years after launch, tying it with The Witcher 3 as one of gaming's all-time best sellers.

items.title: Fallout 4 Sales Update: Over 35 Million Copies Sold Worldwide
items.summary: Bethesda updated sales figures for Fallout 4, revealing the game has sold more than 35 million copies worldwide since its November 2015 release.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple unseated Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings as investors reassess the outlook for artificial intelligence.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft for AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit accusing OpenAI and two former employees of stealing trade secrets to develop the iPhone maker's own yet-to-be-unveiled AI hardware.

items.title: Amazon Launches New Satellite Internet Service 'Leo' in South Africa by 2027
items.summary: Amazon announced it will launch its new satellite internet service, Amazon Leo, in South Africa in 2027, expanding its connectivity footprint outside the US.

items.title: Verizon Fios Introduces New 5Gbps Home Plan
items.summary: Verizon Fios launched a new 5Gbps home internet plan, which is more than twice as fast as its previous flagship 2Gbps plan.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Open Android to Rival AI Chatbots and Share Search Data
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to share internal search data with competitors and open its Android operating system to rival AI chatbots.

items.title: TSMC Plans $100 Billion Expansion of US Manufacturing Capacity
items.summary: Major Taiwan chipmaker TSMC announced plans to invest another $100 billion to expand its manufacturing capacity in the United States.

items.title: GameStop Reveals Nearly 10% Ownership of eBay, Pushing for Buyout
items.summary: GameStop disclosed in a regulatory filing that it owns nearly 10% of eBay, signaling its intent to proceed with plans to buy the e-commerce company despite a rejected unsolicited offer.

items.title: U.S. Justice Department Allows Federal Employees to Download TikTok on Government Devices
items.summary: The U.S. Justice Department announced Friday that federal employees can now download the short-video app TikTok on government devices.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide DoD with Billions in Data Center Capacity for AI
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is reportedly in talks to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models.

items.title: Coca-Cola Halts US Operations of fairlife Due to Ransomware Attack
items.summary: Coca-Cola has halted the US operations of its dairy company fairlife following a ransomware attack.

items.title: Microsoft-Owned Bethesda Confirms Fallout 5 Has Entered Preproduction
items.summary: Following turmoil at Xbox, Microsoft-owned Bethesda confirmed that Fallout 5 has officially entered preproduction.

items.title: Japan's OHISAMA Mission Aims to Beam Wireless Electricity from Space to Earth by 2026
items.summary: Japan is building the OHISAMA system to beam wireless electricity from space directly to Earth, with a satellite launch planned by 2026 to demonstrate the technology.
